verses.text: Enquanto a fome continuava, a família de Jacó precisava de mais grãos, mas não podiam voltar ao Egito sem Benjamim ( **42.16** , **20** ). Jacó percebeu que estava em uma situação difícil; ele precisava de mais comida, mas não queria perder Benjamim.\n\n

Judá quebrou o impasse assumindo a responsabilidade pelo bem-estar de Benjamim, assim sendo bem-sucedido onde Rúben falhou. Sua ação foi apropriada, já que tinha sido sua ideia vender José aos ismaelitas ( **37.26-27** ) em vez de matá-lo. Agora, ele garantiria a segurança de Benjamim com sua própria vida (veja **44.18-34** ).

Agora, ele garantiria a segurança de Benjamim com sua própria vida (veja **44.18-34** ).
Jacó providenciou tudo o que era possível para garantir uma recepção favorável do governador egípcio (o homem ; cp. **32.13-21** ).
Jacó providenciou tudo o que era possível para garantir uma recepção favorável do governador egípcio (o homem ; cp. **32.13-21** ).

Jacó finalmente confiou o futuro de sua família ao Deus Todo-Poderoso , o título divino que enfatiza o poder de Deus (veja também **17.1** ; **28.3** ; **35.11** ; **48.3** ; **1Rs 19.10** , **14** ; **Ap 21.22** ).

Jacó finalmente confiou o futuro de sua família ao Deus Todo-Poderoso , o título divino que enfatiza o poder de Deus (veja também **17.1** ; **28.3** ; **35.11** ; **48.3** ; **1Rs 19.10** , **14** ; **Ap 21.22** ).

43:15 ARRIVAL IN EGYPT. (Gen. 43:15-30) stood before Joseph—We may easily imagine the delight with which, amid the crowd of other applicants, the eye of Joseph would fix on his brethren and Benjamin. But occupied with his public duties, he consigned them to the care of a confidential servant till he should have finished the business of the day.
Quando José viu Benjamim com eles: José agora sabia que Benjamim estava bem (veja nota de estudo em 42.1–44.34 ). O banquete foi tanto uma celebração quanto um teste.

43:17 And the men were afraid, because they were brought into Joseph's house,.... It not being usual, as Jarchi observes, for those that came to buy corn to lodge there, but at an inn in the city: and they said, because of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in; to examine and inquire of them how they came to go away without paying for their corn, take up their money again after they had laid it down, and take it away with them, and so were guilty of tricking and defrauding, if not of theft: that he may seek occasion against us: or "roll on us" (z);
Os irmãos estavam aterrorizados: Cp. **32.6-12** . A culpa deles não os deixava ver que algo bom poderia acontecer.
Os irmãos, completamente vulneráveis e sentindo o peso do julgamento de Deus em suas consciências, se aproximaram do administrador em desespero.
Os irmãos, completamente vulneráveis e sentindo o peso do julgamento de Deus em suas consciências, se aproximaram do administrador em desespero.
Os irmãos, completamente vulneráveis e sentindo o peso do julgamento de Deus em suas consciências, se aproximaram do administrador em desespero.
Os irmãos, completamente vulneráveis e sentindo o peso do julgamento de Deus em suas consciências, se aproximaram do administrador em desespero.

43:23 And he said - The address of the steward in this verse plainly proves that the knowledge of the true God was in Egypt. It is probable that the steward himself was a Hebrew, and that Joseph had given him intimation of the whole affair; and though he was not at liberty to reveal it, yet he gives them assurances that the whole business would issue happily. I had your money - כספכם בא אלי caspechem ba elai, your money comes to me.
José não tratava mais seus irmãos com hostilidade, pelo contrário, oferecia hospitalidade amável.

43:25 And when Joseph came home,.... In order to dine, it being noontime: they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house; everyone took a part of it in his hand, and brought it to Joseph in the parlour where he was, and delivered it to him as a present from their father, or from themselves, or it may be as from both: and bowed themselves to him to the earth; in the most prostrate and humble manner, now again fulfilling his dream, and more completely than before, for now all his eleven brethren were together, signified by the eleven stars in the dream, that made obeisance to him, see Gen 37:9.
Pela segunda vez, os irmãos se curvaram... diante de José em cumprimento de seu primeiro sonho ( **37.7** ; veja **42.6-7** ; cf. **33.3** ).

43:27 And he asked them of their welfare - This verse may be thus translated: "And he asked them concerning their prosperity; and he said, is your father prosperous, the old man who ye told me was alive? And they said, Thy servant our father prospers; he is yet alive."

43:28 And he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother Benjamin,.... He had seen him before when his brethren first presented themselves to him, but then took no particular and special notice of him, only gave him a side look as it were, but now he looked wistly at him: his mother's son; the son of Rachel his mother, and who was his only brother by his mother's side, the rest, though his brethren, yet only by his father's side, not his mother's sons: and said, is this your younger brother, of whom ye spake unto me?
Que Deus seja gracioso com você, meu filho: A bênção de José para seu irmão de sangue cumpriu a oração de Jacó ( **43.14** ).

ele foi dominado pela emoção por seu irmão: Cp. **42.24** . As lágrimas de José eram de memórias dolorosas e anos de convivência perdida, bem como de alegria e gratidão por ver seu irmão novamente. • Ele foi para seu quarto privado: Ele ainda não planejava revelar sua identidade.

43:31 THE DINNER. (Gen 43:31-34) Joseph said, Set on bread—equivalent to having dinner served, "bread" being a term inclusive of all victuals. The table was a small stool, most probably the usual round form, "since persons might even then be seated according to their rank or seniority, and the modern Egyptian table is not without its post of honor and a fixed gradation of place" [WILKINSON]. Two or at most three persons were seated at one table. But the host being the highest in rank of the company had a table to himself;

43:32 Egyptians might not eat bread with the Hebrews; for that is an abomination—The prejudice probably arose from the detestation in which, from the oppressions of the shepherd-kings, the nation held all of that occupation.
para seu espanto: Este governante sabia mais sobre os irmãos do que parecia possível (cp. **44.15** ).

Os irmãos foram confrontados com atos generosos e graciosos de Deus através de José, que testou sua tolerância ao reencenar o favoritismo pelo filho de Raquel que havia galvanizado sua hostilidade anterior.
